verses.2: Don’t hide your face from me in the day of my distress.
verses.3: For my days consume away like smoke.
verses.4: My heart is blighted like grass, and withered,
verses.5: By reason of the voice of my groaning,
verses.6: I am like a pelican of the wilderness.
verses.7: I watch, and have become like a sparrow that is alone on the housetop.
verses.8: My enemies reproach me all day.
verses.9: For I have eaten ashes like bread,
verses.10: because of your indignation and your wrath;
verses.11: My days are like a long shadow.
verses.12: But you, LORD, will remain forever;
verses.13: You will arise and have mercy on Zion,
verses.14: For your servants take pleasure in her stones,
verses.15: So the nations will fear the LORD’s name,
verses.16: For the LORD has built up Zion.
verses.17: He has responded to the prayer of the destitute,
verses.18: This will be written for the generation to come.
verses.19: for he has looked down from the height of his sanctuary.
verses.20: to hear the groans of the prisoner,
verses.21: that men may declare the LORD’s name in Zion,
verses.22: when the peoples are gathered together,
verses.23: He weakened my strength along the course.
verses.24: I said, “My God, don’t take me away in the middle of my days.
verses.25: Of old, you laid the foundation of the earth.
verses.26: They will perish, but you will endure.
verses.28: The children of your servants will continue.
